channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Former FBI Director Robert Mueller dies at 81; Trump reacts
item.description: Robert Mueller, former FBI director and special counsel in the Russia investigation, has died at age 81, and President Donald Trump reacted harshly online.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge orders DHS to open Whipple to clergy
item.description: A federal judge in St. Paul on Friday ordered the Department of Homeland Security to develop a plan to ensure that clergy are able to visit immigration detainees at the Whipple Federal Building.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Chicago's teetering debt is stark warning left-wing mayor is fueling 'pay later' doom cycle: expert
item.description: Expert Austin Berg warns Chicago's "pay later" debt culture under Mayor Brandon Johnson is alarming markets with a $1 billion budget gap and risky bond deals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Consumer safeguards gain bipartisan support at the Capitol
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst talks about proposals to protect consumers from scams and other ways Minnesotan’s might be swindled. Plus, a check-in with the state’s economic development commissioner.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: MAHA and the Trump administration’s deregulatory agenda
item.description: The Make America Healthy Again movement has sounded the alarm on environmental toxins, but the Trump administration has pushed to roll back regulations. MPR News guest host Kelly Gordon talks about the mismatch with nutritionist, Marion Nestle.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Gordon and Gabrielle Zuckerman, MPR News

item.title: Mullin tells senators Homeland Security needs funding as he faces confirmation hearing
item.description: Markwayne Mullin is telling senators he wants to steady the Department of Homeland Security, fund it and keep it out of constant controversy. The Republican Oklahoma senator appeared Wednesday for his confirmation hearing as President Donald Trump’s pick to lead DHS after Kristi Noem was fired.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Walz puts social media company tax into budget plan
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz proposed levying a tax on social media companies to counteract economic disruptions from artificial intelligence. Walz also wants relief payments to those affected by federal immigration actions, as well as more child care refunds and affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump demands NATO and China police the Strait of Hormuz. So far they aren't joining
item.description: With the Iran war entering a third week, Israel said it plans for at least three more weeks of war, while President Trump demanded other countries help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Psychedelic medicine: What the research says and what comes next
item.description: MPR News host Catharine Richert and her guests talk about the science of psychedelic medicine and the policy debate over whether it should become legal therapy.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Israel says it killed 2 top Iranian commanders in a targeted strike
item.description: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani, the highest-profile assassinations since the targeting of Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, on the first day of the war.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Report: Minnesota to see billions in climate costs
item.description: The Minnesota Pollution Control Agency report found that the costs of inaction, including health effects and damaged infrastructure, far exceed the cost of climate adaptation measures.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear expedited arguments on TPS
item.description: The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.
item.dc:creator: Zoe Sobel and Nina Totenberg, MPR News
